item.title: No vote for me
item.content:encoded: This was pretty interesting at first, but for me it got a bit to boring, and I found it hard to finish. I'm sure there are people who like it, but it's just not for me. No vote

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
What I expected: some kind of play on the infinite monkeys and typewriters thing

What I got: weird fat fetish fanfic

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: Took me a bit to understand the meaning behind this, near the end it clicked. And so I clicked +1

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: description says broadcasts occur on a random day in december but all the dates are listed as the 11th month november.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
I have some theories:

  1. The transmissions end
  2. It just goes into the negative because why not
  3. Some form of containment breach.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: True. Except the last one, possibly, which apparently occurs on 11/31/14…despite there being only 30 days in November. Wut?

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: Will anything happen in 2024? That is when the final orangutan is shot, after all

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: the orangautans will be sad:(

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: Another skip that tries to destroy humanity because of what they've done to Earth, specifically the animals. Cool logs though. Made me look up "On the Evils of Tobacco" which I can't find a copy of online.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: Oh, delightfully weird, and upvoted. One issue, though: in the text, it says the broadcasts occur on a random day in December, yet all the dates seem to be for November.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
Good stuff, this is another of the radio SCP's that Zyn recommended and she ain't wrong.

I actually don't remember recommending this specifically, especially since I didn't vote on this yet.

That said, OP, have an upvote! Those logs are… well. Damn.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
Good stuff, this is another of the radio SCP's that Zyn recommended and she ain't wrong.

It's weird and random in a disturbing Lychian way, and I like how you can see the effects of the brainwashing starting to take shape in the Foundation throughout. I also like how there's threads such as the D-Class log which could have some connection to what's going on, but we'll never really know.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: You do insanity like no one else. +1.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
I was gonna say this was the craziest thing I'd read on this site since 3999, but well look who done wrote it. :D It's a shame a true audio adaptation would get copyright-flagged, because I'd love to hear one.

Thanks, now I'm afraid of orangutans.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: Something about your style of weird really gets to me. This is the stuff of nightmares, man. +1

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: well… umm.. okay then… this was strange. Strange in a good way, but strange. Im curious to know what will happen once they reach zero orangutans. Then again, I might not want to know…

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: Yea, those logs fucked me right up. +1

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
No vote.

In the last line of the first paragraph of the Special Containment Procedures, I think you mean uninhibited instead of uninhabited. I'm not 100% sure, so I didn't change it.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
The hell?

No vote because I just didn't get it, it also didn't really seem like keter material (should be nerfed to Euclid) and the similarities to another skip (it was in series 3 somewhere)

EDIT SCP-2078

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
But… what happens when they run out of orangutans?

Wow, this was trippy. I love it. +1.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: Good to see you back, LordStonefish. This was deliciously unsettling (pun intended). I'll be giving it another read through, the first one was almost overwhelming.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: The broadcasts were pretty good non-sequiter horror. I might be reading into this too much, but is there a connection between this and SCP-2078?

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
(Conversation between two people, one torturing the other. Voices identified as Irene Rosenfeld, CEO of Mondelez International and Robert Siegel, radio journalist and host of National Public Radio's "All Things Considered")

I laughed audibly at this. However with that said I just didn't dig the rest of it. It's never really Keter material, especially since it can be effectively rendered inert by playing a stronger signal of static. It needs some work

-1

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
This radio broadcast is what I was always hoping to hear cruising through static as a kid, or what people who listen to numbers stations daydream about running across. Something that's actually confidential but packaged in a form where it's barely recognizable to those who don't already know it.

Also the counting down orangutans got progressively scarier.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: That's essentially right on track with what's going on.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
Body positivity is deprogramming the thought that only certain bodies are worth acceptance and romantic love, and instead recognizing that all bodies are equally valuable to the economy.

How could I not upvote this?

It's hard to tell what's going on in this - my interpretation was

+ spoiler
– hide block

the Foundation is losing their collective mind, whatever those broadcasts are doing is totally working on them and now they're overfeeding everybody on Mondelez products and influence


but it could be a bunch of other things just as easily, which fits with the overall weirdness here. The broadcasts were just the right balance of creepiness and gibberish for me.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: Eh, I dunno. Just wanting to pop something off. I like this though.
